analystDecision: Press the best longs, but keep entries disciplined.
riskPosture: Risk is not gone. Respect the trim list and do not turn watchlist names into forced trades.
operatorSummary: The board reads constructive risk-on, with 6 buy setups and 3 risk-reduction names. Current evaluation history shows 100.0% 1D hit rate, so the edge looks real but still young.
nextSessionPlan: Open with AMD, MRVL, SMCI on the main board, keep SPCX, SYM marked for risk control, and let weaker holds prove themselves before touching them.

newsItems.title: S&P 500, Dow Snap Three-Day Losses, Nasdaq Ends Best Day In Three Weeks As Earnings Take Centerstage — GOOGL, AAPL, AMD, TSLA, DIS In Focus
newsItems.summary: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index jumped over 5%, its best single-day gain in a month, as bargain buyers piled onto semiconductor stocks.

newsItems.title: Stocks to Watch: GM, BAE Systems, Novartis, Swatch
newsItems.summary: ↗️ Novartis (CH:NOVN): Shares in the Swiss pharmaceutical company climbed 2% after it returned to sales growth in the second quarter, driven by some of its top-selling drugs. ↗️ General Motors (GM): The automaker raised its earnings guidance.

newsItems.title: Dow Jones Futures: S&P 500 Regains 50-Day As Sandisk, Micron Soar; Google, Tesla Earnings Due
newsItems.summary: The S&P 500 reclaimed key support as Sandisk, Micron and AI stocks led a broad rally. Google, Tesla earnings loom.

newsItems.title: Supermicro (SMCI) Faces ITC Patent Probe Over Memory Imports
newsItems.summary: The U.S. International Trade Commission has opened a new investigation into alleged patent infringement of memory technologies. Super Micro Computer (NasdaqGS:SMCI) is named as a respondent, alongside other large technology companies. Complainant Netlist is seeking exclusion and

newsItems.title: Jamie Dimon throws caution on the AI frenzy but sees promise in space data centers
newsItems.summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has his doubts that the AI spending boom will turn out the way investors are hoping for. Still, he's willing to entertain one of the AI build-out's more extraordinary projects: SpaceX's ambition to put data centers in space.

newsItems.title: Mark Cuban has strong words on income and inequality
newsItems.summary: Juan Hernandez joined SpaceX in 2015 as a welder making $28 an hour. The company offered him $10,000 in stock alongside his wages. He accepted without giving it much thought.
